summary: Reporter encounters an AccessViolationException crash in SKCodec.GetScaledDimensions on Windows with SkiaSharp 2.88.3, and self-identifies the issue as a duplicate of #2645.

stepsToReproduce: Load an image file into a MemoryStream

repoLinks.description: Duplicate: same AccessViolationException in SKCodec.GetScaledDimensions, closed

versionAnalysis.relevanceReason: Issue #2645 (duplicate) was closed; unclear if fix was applied to 2.88.3 line.

regression.reason: Reporter states last known good version is 2.88.2, crash occurs in 2.88.3.

analysis.summary: AccessViolationException crash in SKCodec.GetScaledDimensions on certain images. The reporter self-identified this as a duplicate of #2645, which reported the same crash between 2.88.5 and 2.88.6. The C# code at SKCodec.cs:42-47 calls sk_codec_get_scaled_dimensions directly via P/Invoke with no null or validity guard.
analysis.rationale: Reporter explicitly states this is a duplicate of #2645 (same AccessViolationException in GetScaledDimensions). Both issues report a regression between minor 2.88.x versions. The crash is in native code accessed via P/Invoke, suggesting a native-side bug introduced in Skia.

keySignals.text: I got crash in SKCodec > GetScaledDimensions with AccessViolation exception
keySignals.interpretation: Native memory access violation during codec scaled dimensions query.

keySignals.text: Sorry I just see that its a duplicate of https://github.com/mono/SkiaSharp/issues/2645

codeInvestigation.finding: GetScaledDimensions calls sk_codec_get_scaled_dimensions via P/Invoke with no null/validity guard on Handle. If the codec was created from corrupted or unsupported image data, the native call may dereference an invalid pointer.

nextQuestions: Was #2645 actually fixed in a subsequent release?
Does the crash occur on specific image formats or all images?
Is the codec handle valid when GetScaledDimensions is called (i.e., did SKCodec.Create return non-null)?

actionability.reason: Reporter self-confirmed duplicate of #2645, which describes the same AccessViolationException in the same API.

actions.description: Apply bug, core SkiaSharp, Windows platform labels
